user.title: Ecobee (Anderson fork)
user.description:
Sign in with your ecobee.com account.

This fork bypasses ecobee's shut-down developer portal by using your account password directly. If your account has two-factor authentication (2FA) enabled, you'll be prompted for your second-factor code on the next step.

reauth_confirm.title: Reauthenticate Ecobee
reauth_confirm.description: The session for {username} has expired. Enter your password to refresh it. If 2FA is enabled, you'll be asked for your second-factor code on the next step.

mfa_select_factor.title: Choose a verification method
mfa_select_factor.description: Your ecobee account ({username}) has multiple two-factor methods configured. Pick which one you want to use.

mfa_code.title: Enter your verification code
mfa_code.description: Enter the verification code from {factor} for the account {username}. For an authenticator app, use the current 6-digit code; for SMS, use the code that was just texted to you.

error.mfa_unsupported: This MFA method isn't supported yet (push notifications can't be wired through this integration). Configure an authenticator app or SMS as a backup factor on your ecobee account and try again.
error.mfa_no_factors: Your ecobee account requires 2FA but no usable verification methods were returned. Open ecobee.com -> account security and confirm at least one authenticator (app or SMS) is active, then try again.
error.mfa_invalid_code: That code didn't match. Try again.
error.mfa_expired: Your verification session expired. Re-enter your email and password to start over.
error.mfa_rate_limited: Too many incorrect codes. Wait a few minutes before trying again.
error.mfa_push_unsupported: Push-notification MFA is not yet supported by this integration. Pick an authenticator app or SMS factor instead.
error.auth0_unreachable: Could not reach auth.ecobee.com. Check the HA host's outbound network/DNS.
error.internal: Unexpected error. Check Settings -> System -> Logs for the full traceback.
